metric_value: Using clock-speed GHz equivalency ratings pegged to Intel Pentium 4
notes: AMD named Athlon XP models using numbers implying GHz equivalence to Intel Pentium 4

metric_value: Assumes specific usage model not applicable to many users
notes: Flaw 1: benchmark suite reflects narrow workloads; not representative of typical buyer usage

metric_value: Ignores critical platform differences including memory type
notes: Flaw 2: results vary by memory (DDR vs RDRAM) and other platform factors not disclosed

metric_value: Inconsistent ratings between mobile and desktop processor lines
notes: Flaw 3: same PR number means different things for mobile vs. desktop Athlon XP

metric_name: Methodology flaw: I/O in processor benchmark
metric_value: Uses system-level I/O benchmarks not used by industry for processor-only measurement
notes: Flaw 4: including I/O in benchmark inflates apparent processor performance vs. CPU-only tests

metric_name: Aberdeen characterization of AMD's GHz-E approach
notes: Kastner explicitly labels methodology 'bad science'; uses 'slippery slope' framing

metric_value: Inexperienced buyers accept the misleading ratings
notes: Title frames target buyers as 'inexperienced'; implies sophisticated buyers would reject the claims

metric_name: Sustainability of GHz Equivalency naming methodology
metric_value: Methodology is fundamentally flawed and unlikely to be sustained
notes: Kastner's critique implies the PR system cannot be maintained without damaging AMD's credibility

metric_name: AMD abandonment of Athlon XP PR numbering system
metric_value: AMD launched Athlon 64 in September 2003 without GHz-Equivalency PR ratings
notes: Athlon 64 used actual clock speeds (e.g., 3200+ referred to physical speed); PR system quietly dropped

metric_name: Intel Pentium 4 as GHz marketing benchmark
metric_value: Reference platform for AMD's equivalency claims
notes: Intel's GHz-first Pentium 4 marketing created pressure on AMD to respond with GHz-like ratings

metric_value: Dominant clock-speed marketing set market expectation AMD had to counter
notes: Intel Pentium 4 NetBurst architecture achieved higher clock speeds at cost of IPC; created GHz arms race

metric_name: Athlon XP actual clock speed vs. branded model number
metric_value: Athlon XP 2100+ runs at 1733 MHz; implies equivalence to Intel Pentium 4 at 2100 MHz
notes: Gap between physical GHz and branded PR number is central to the misleading-marketing claim

metric_value: Processor-only benchmarks; I/O not included in CPU measurement
notes: Kastner notes industry does not include I/O in CPU benchmarks — AMD's methodology violates this norm
